narrative: OMX30 opens into mixed European sentiment with DAX outperforming CAC and FTSE, while US equities finished soft (NASDAQ -0.71%) on rising Treasury yields now at 4.463%. The strong USD/SEK at 9.323 (+1.16%) and EUR/SEK at 10.92 (+0.58%) should support large-cap exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson), but gains are capped by technical weakness: MACD below signal, ADX showing moderate strength with bearish DI bias, and the index still -5.4% from 52W highs. Copper's +2.04% surge is a bright spot for Boliden, but oil's stability and gold's modest +0.40% offer limited additional commodity tailwind. The index trades 6.5% above 200-MA in established bull trend, yet sits only +0.1% above 50-MA with RSI in neutral territory—classic setup for choppy, range-bound trade.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a cautious open on Wednesday with mixed global signals and conflicting technical messages. DAX's +0.75% carry provides some upside impetus, but NASDAQ's -0.71% close and the sharp rise in US 10Y yields (now 4.463%) limit enthusiasm for Swedish equities. Key tailwind: USD/SEK at 9.323 is a 14-month high, favoring large exporters—watch Volvo B, Atlas Copco A, and Ericsson B for outperformance. Headwind: ADX at 22.2 with bearish DI bias and MACD below signal suggest limited directional follow-through; trade cautiously above 3180 (50-MA). Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) should see modest support from yield steepness, but real estate faces refinancing pressure. Boliden watch: copper +2.04% is a solid long driver. Expect range trade 3085–3180; break above 3180 needed to justify fresh longs. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could spark afternoon volatility.
drivers: DAX strength (+0.75%) and European industrial resilience offset NASDAQ weakness
USD/SEK surge (+1.16%) supports exporters but copper strength mixed signal
US 10Y yield spike (+1.20%) positive for banks but headwind for real estate and tech
RSI 39.66 neutral; MACD below signal and ADX 22.2 moderate with bearish DI bias suggests limited conviction

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK strength (+1.16% to 9.323) and DAX momentum (+0.75%) boost export pricing and order flow; however, ADX bearish DI bias and MACD weakness temper conviction.
US 10Y yield spike to 4.463% (+1.20%) widens net interest margin expectations for Swedish banks despite modest local rate impact.
NASDAQ -0.71% close and rising US yields create DCF headwind; partially offset by strong USD/SEK for Ericsson export pricing.
US 10Y yield +1.20% and rising Swedish real rates increase refinancing costs and cap property valuations.
Copper +2.04% and gold +0.40% provide direct revenue tailwinds; strong momentum despite neutral overall technical backdrop.
Risk-off sentiment from NASDAQ weakness and yield rises typically support defensives, but global sentiment remains slightly constructive (DAX +0.75%); rotational bid rather than urgent flight.
Weak EUR/SEK (up to 10.92) increases H&M sourcing costs; stable oil and modest risk aversion limit consumer discretionary momentum.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.04% and gold +0.40% offer direct revenue uplift; strong commodity tailwind despite neutral index technicals.
USD/SEK at 9.323 (+1.16%) supports export margins; DAX +0.75% adds cyclical tailwind.
NASDAQ -0.71% and rising US yields create sentiment headwind; USD/SEK strength helps but insufficient to offset tech sell-off.
US 10Y +1.20% to 4.463% widens NIM; modest Swedish rate sensitivity positive.
EUR/SEK weakness (up to 10.92) increases sourcing costs; weak consumer sentiment and risk aversion limit upside.

keyLevels.support: 3085 (lower Bollinger band; %B=0.22 indicates lower half of range)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims (initial jobless claims) at 14:30 CET—potential volatility driver given elevated yield sensitivity.
topRisk: MACD below signal and ADX with bearish DI bias suggest range-bound trade or downside retest; reversal below 3085 could trigger stop cascades. US data disappointment at 14:30 CET could spike VIX (currently 17.89, -0.56%) and pressure all risk assets. Trade-war headlines (UK Labour leadership) and geopolitical escalation remain tail risks.
technicalSummary: Above 200-MA (+6.5%) in bull trend but +0.1% above 50-MA shows lack of momentum near-term
MACD below signal line with hist=-7.426 and bearish DI bias in ADX 22.2 (moderate) — distribution not complete but conviction weak
RSI 39.66 neutral, Stochastic K=25.7 D=31.5 oversold-to-neutral — no mean reversion urgency yet
Bollinger %B=0.22 in lower half, width 5.6% — volatility modest, support at 3085 credible
ROC-90 +5.7% confirms strong 3-month momentum; 52W at 78% of range with -5.4% gap to highs — room to run but technical confirmation needed
